# SRC059 - FTC Operation AI Comply / Deceptive AI Claims

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Platform Policy |
| Authors / source | FTC |
| Year | 2024/current |
| Source type | Regulator enforcement guidance |
| Quality | High |
| URL | [https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2024/09/ftc-announces-crackdown-deceptive-ai-claims-schemes](https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2024/09/ftc-announces-crackdown-deceptive-ai-claims-schemes) |

## Bias / limitation

Regulator enforcement; US context.

## Core takeaway

AI claims do not get a pass; deceptive AI hype can trigger enforcement.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use to make AI capability claims evidence-bound.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.
